hero.name: Andreas Gabel
hero.title: Datamatiker · DevOps · Infrastructure & Network
hero.tagline: Removing friction, not just writing code. Most projects here run as live, containerized services on a single server I operate, deploy, and document end-to-end.

about.heading: About Me
about.p1: I'm a 4th semester Computer Science (Datamatiker) student at EK in Copenhagen, focused on DevOps, automation, and infrastructure — not as theory, but as something that solves real problems for real people.
about.p2: I work in IT Operations at Københavns Kommune, managing Linux-based clients, Windows workstations, networking, and infrastructure across 7 citizen service locations. That has taught me what it means to have systems in production — with real users, real consequences, and very little margin for error.
about.p3: This portfolio is more than a list of projects — most applications run live on a single server, containerized with Docker, routed through Nginx, deployed via CI/CD pipelines, and scanned with Trivy. The infrastructure itself is part of the portfolio.

items.title: IT Operations & Infrastructure
items.description: Managing Linux-based clients, Windows workstations, and network infrastructure across 7 citizen service locations. Patch panels, switching, hardware provisioning, and test environments. Automating internal processes and removing friction from daily operations.

items.title: Computer Science (Datamatiker)
items.description: Software development with Java, Spring Boot, and databases — with a personal focus on DevOps, CI/CD, containerization, and infrastructure automation. Currently in 4th semester.

items.title: Teacher — Special Needs Education
items.description: Taught science and religious studies to students with autism. Liaison between municipal agencies and the school, ensuring the best learning environment for young people with extra challenges.

items.title: Theology
items.org: University of Copenhagen — Faculty of Theology
items.description: Studied theology. Active member of the board of 'Teobar' and 'Teologisk Vandrelaug'.

items.title: Naval Officer Cadet
items.description: Training in leadership, navigation, meteorology, ship manoeuvring, and maritime law. Completed sergeant education and was appointed sergeant. Responsible for voyage planning and participated in multiple voyages at sea.

contact.heading: Get in Touch
contact.text: Interested in working together or just want to say hello? Feel free to reach out.
